channel.title: Practice on Eau Claire Buddhist Sangha
channel.description: Recent content in Practice on Eau Claire Buddhist Sangha

item.title: Tips for Meditation Posture
item.description:
After struggling with lower back pain during sits for almost two years, I’ve finally found a setup that works. Thought I’d share in case it helps others.

What helped me

  1. A higher cushion — I switched from a standard zafu to a taller one (about 6 inches). This tilted my pelvis forward enough that my spine could stack naturally.

  2. A folded blanket under my knees — For those of us who sit cross-legged, a little support under the knees takes pressure off the hips.

item.title: Daily Practice Struggles
item.description:
How do you all maintain a 20-minute daily sit when the kids are home? I’ve been struggling to find consistent time in the mornings before work.

I’ve tried:

  • Waking up 30 minutes earlier (hard when the kids wake up early too)
  • Sitting in the evening (I’m too tired)
  • Short 10-minute sits (helpful but not the same)

Would love to hear what works for other parents in the sangha.
